title: carex brevior var. molesta
intro: Carex brevior var. molesta, also known as bitter sedge, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its bitter and astringent properties, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to possess health benefits such as aiding in digestion, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
